steps.title: Isolate a
steps.content: Starting from $x = 8a(b+9)$, divide both sides by $8(b+9)$ (nonzero since $b$ is positive): $$a = \frac{x}{8(b+9)}.$$

steps.title: Eliminate the answer choices
steps.content:
• A gives $a = \frac{x}{8} - (b+9)$, which would only follow if the original equation were $x = 8(a + b + 9)$ — not the given product form.
  • B gives $a = \frac{x}{8(b+9)}$, exactly what we derived.
  • C gives $a = \frac{8(b+9)}{x}$, the reciprocal of B and not equivalent.
  • D gives $a = 8x(b+9)$, which would multiply rather than divide by $8(b+9)$.
That is the correct answer.
